data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review — criterion E4 (assumption/premise verification). I probed every codebase assertion in the plan via Grep/Read rather than relying on cached knowledge.

AFFIRMATIONS (assertions verified true against source):
- "~96 pre-seeded `# noqa: BLE001`" — VERIFIED approximately: the joe-janitor audit (.joe-janitor/report-2026-06-24.md:89) reports "~95 carry `# noqa: BLE001`"; the plan's "~96" (hedged with "~") is within tolerance.
- The error-handling convention text (raise-don't-catch on MCP, stdout-sacred / T201 banned in library/core/MCP but allowed in the CLI presentation layer + reconciler stderr, named-logger-to-stderr substrate, observability floor + `# noqa: BLE001`-with-reason) — VERIFIED grounded in src/rebar/_logging.py:25-50 (the module docstring states each of these).
- "The error-handling convention is captured in `CLAUDE.md` / a contributing doc" is NOT-YET-true — VERIFIED ABSENT: grep for "stdout is sacred" in CLAUDE.md and "raise-don't-catch" tree-wide return zero hits, and docs/ has no error-handling-convention file. This is consistent with the plan's own "document durably" task (a thing-to-do, not a false premise) — PASS, not a finding.
- "`_io/` is now owned by the core-leaves sweep `crow-aeon-muck`, so it can actually be removed from the allowlist" — VERIFIED: ticket 8d01 (crow-aeon-muck) landed with pyproject.toml file_impact "remove core-leaves entries from the shrinking allowlist" and re-validated the 7 `_io/import_ndjson.py` BLE001 inline noqas; pyproject.toml:207-210 confirms the core-leaves (incl. _io) were swept by 8d01 and removed.
- "Their fate depends on the reconciler sweep (`colder-bugle-rake`)" — VERIFIED: ticket 0569 (colder-bugle-rake) `blocks` this ticket (0ec1), and landed keeping T201 / removing BLE001 for the reconciler package.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] [E4] The plan asserts "all 42 [T201 annotations] live in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/`, NOT the CLI" but BOTH the count and the "all" are contradicted by source: a scoped grep of `# noqa: T201` under src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/ returns 41 sites (not 42), and pyproject.toml:201 itself says "the reconciler's 41 sites". Moreover inline `# noqa: T201` ALSO exist OUTSIDE the reconciler — graph/_links.py:190 (stdout redirect-record data contract) and 7 sites in _io/_cli.py:72-126 (CLI presentation) — so "all 42 live in the reconciler" is false. Correct the count to 41 and reword to "the 41 reconciler-subprocess T201 sites" (acknowledging the graph/_links + _io/_cli inline T201 are separate, kept, legitimate-print/data-contract sites).
  criteria: [E4]
  location: "What" bullet 2 + AC2 ("they live in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/`, not the CLI") vs pyproject.toml:201, and grep of `# noqa: T201` (src/rebar/graph/_links.py:190, src/rebar/_io/_cli.py:72,73,87,110,111,117,118,124,126)
  evidence:
    - Scoped grep `# noqa: T201` in src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/ = 41 hits (acli.py, outbound_differ.py, applier.py, batch_dispatch.py, apply_inbound.py, reconcile.py, invariants.py).
    - pyproject.toml:201: "the close-out keeps their justification (inline `# noqa: T201` for the reconciler's 41 sites; ...)" — author's own config says 41.
    - graph/_links.py:190 `print(  # noqa: T201 — stdout data: machine-readable redirect record (CLI contract)` and _io/_cli.py:72-126 (7 sites, "CLI presentation") are inline T201 OUTSIDE the reconciler — so "all 42 live in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/`" is not a true partition.
  suggested_fix: Reword to "41 reconciler-subprocess T201 sites in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/`" and add a clause noting the graph/_links.py:190 stdout-data-contract T201 and the _io/_cli.py CLI-presentation T201s are separate, kept, legitimate-print sites (not part of this reconciliation).

- [ ] [E4] The plan asserts "Empty the `per-file-ignores` allowlist — all packages are conformant" and AC5 says "The BLE001/T201 allowlist is empty (no package exempt — including `_io/`)", but source shows the allowlist canNOT be empty: pyproject.toml:202-233 retains six per-file-ignore entries that are documented as PERMANENT / legitimate-print surfaces, not transitional. Reword to "empty the SHRINKING (transitional) allowlist; the permanent legitimate-print T201 entries (tests, _engine_support, _commands, _alias, reconciler, jira-capability-probe) remain by design".
  criteria: [E4]
  location: "What" bullet 4 + AC5 ("the allowlist is empty (no package exempt)") vs pyproject.toml:202-233
  evidence:
    - pyproject.toml:206 `"tests/**" = ["BLE001", "T201"]` with comment "PERMANENT, not part of the shrinking allowlist."
    - pyproject.toml:216 `"src/rebar/_engine_support/*" = ["T201"]` ("KEEPS T201 — it is the data-CLI presentation layer ... a legitimate-print surface").
    - pyproject.toml:224 `"src/rebar/_commands/*" = ["T201"]`, :225 `"src/rebar/_alias.py" = ["T201"]`, :232 `"src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/*" = ["T201"]` ("a legitimate-print surface"), :233 `"src/rebar/_engine/jira-capability-probe.py" = ["T201"]` — all KEPT T201 ignores, explicitly NOT slated for removal.
    - pyproject.toml:181-182 frames it as a "*shrinking* per-file-ignores allowlist": entries are DELETED as each sweep completes — but the legitimate-print T201 entries above are documented as kept, so "empty (no package exempt)" overstates the end state.
  suggested_fix: Reword the "What" bullet and AC5 to "the SHRINKING (transitional) BLE001/T201 allowlist is emptied — every transitional exemption is removed; the documented permanent legitimate-print T201 entries (tests/**, _engine_support, _commands, _alias, rebar_reconciler, jira-capability-probe) remain". This also resolves the apparent contradiction with the plan's own "keep the legitimate reconciler-subprocess T201 prints" bullet.

Both findings are premise-accuracy/verifiability corrections (the remediation intent is sound and the dependency wiring on crow-aeon-muck/colder-bugle-rake checks out); they are not blockers.
